channel.title: Frontion Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇷🛢️ Iran Offers to Reopen Hormuz in Exchange for End to US Blockade
item.description: Iran has proposed reopening the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts its naval blockade and ends the war, while postponing nuclear discussions. Secretary of State Marco Rubio rejected any deal that excludes Iran's nuclear program, as Brent crude closed above $108/barrel.

item.title: 🏛️🔬 Trump Administration Fires Entire National Science Board
item.description: All 22 members of the National Science Board, the independent body overseeing the National Science Foundation, received termination notices from the Presidential Personnel Office. Scientists warn the move could enable deep cuts to fundamental research funding.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇦🇫 Pakistan Accused of Airstrikes on Afghan University
item.description: Taliban officials say Pakistani attacks on Kunar province killed at least 7 people and injured 75, including students at Kunar University. Pakistan denied the reports. The attacks threaten a fragile ceasefire brokered by China in April.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan on alert as Chinese warships approach Penghu Islands
item.description: Taiwan detected a Chinese destroyer and frigate operating near the Penghu islands, home to major Taiwanese military bases, prompting Taipei to deploy naval and air forces to monitor the vessels.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German Chancellor links Ukraine peace to territorial concessions
item.description: Friedrich Merz suggested Ukraine may need to cede territory to Russia as part of a peace deal, framing such concessions as a potential pathway to EU membership for Kyiv.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea opens memorial for troops killed in Ukraine war
item.description: Kim Jong Un inaugurated a memorial museum honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, pledging continued support for Moscow's war effort during a ceremony attended by Russia's defense minister.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo meets first female Archbishop of Canterbury in historic encounter
item.description: Pope Leo XIV prayed with Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally at the Vatican, vowing to overcome differences in a historic meeting with the first female leader of the Church of England and the global Anglican Communion.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin pledges support for Iran as foreign minister visits Moscow
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin promised to support Iran during talks with Foreign Minister Araghchi, who said Tehran is considering Trump's request for negotiations while claiming Washington has 'achieved none of their goals' in the war.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanese President Aoun warns against 'traitors' dragging country into war
item.description: President Joseph Aoun condemned those who would involve Lebanon in foreign conflicts, vowing accountability for anyone dragging the nation into war to serve foreign interests amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia to double fuel reserves with \$800M strategic plan
item.description: Coalition government announces major investment in fuel stockpiles to strengthen energy security, with strategic reserves designed to protect against supply disruptions and geopolitical shocks.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian foreign minister heads to Russia as Trump calls for negotiations
item.description: Abbas Araghchi traveled to Moscow for consultations as Trump expressed willingness to negotiate, though the administration canceled peace envoy travel to Pakistan and demands Iran abandon nuclear ambitions.

item.title: 🇧🇭 Bahrain revokes citizenship of 69 people over support for Iranian attacks
item.description: The Gulf kingdom stripped citizenship from dozens of individuals accused of supporting Iranian military operations, escalating regional crackdown on Tehran-linked networks amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🏛️ US Supreme Court reinstates Republican-favored Texas electoral map
item.description: The Supreme Court lifted a lower court ruling that had blocked the map, allowing Republicans to use districts that critics say dilute minority voting power ahead of midterm elections.

item.title: 💰 Dollar advances as US-Iran talks suffer setback
item.description: The US dollar strengthened after peace negotiations appeared to falter, with markets responding to geopolitical uncertainty and ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles begins US state visit amid Iran tensions and security concerns
item.description: King Charles III arrived at the White House for a four-day state visit overshadowed by UK-US disagreements over the Iran war and fallout from the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting. Trump praised the monarch while continuing to criticize PM Starmer.

item.title: ⛪ Pope and first female Archbishop of Canterbury meet and pray together
item.description: Pope Leo XIV and Archbishop Sarah Mullally held a historic meeting at the Vatican, marking a significant moment for Catholic-Anglican relations as Mullally became the first woman to lead the Church of England.

item.title: ⚔️ IDF chief criticizes 'unethical incidents' by soldiers
item.description: Lt. Gen. Herzi Halevi said military discipline has eroded during recent conflicts, vowing accountability for misconduct by Israeli forces fighting in Gaza and Lebanon.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China blocks Meta's \$2B acquisition of AI startup Manus
item.description: Chinese regulators rejected Facebook parent company Meta's planned purchase of the AI startup, tightening control over the artificial intelligence sector amid intensifying geopolitical competition with the US.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan completes US Abrams tank deal with final shipment
item.description: The last batch of M1A2 Abrams tanks arrived in Taiwan, completing a major arms purchase from the United States as the island strengthens its defenses against potential Chinese aggression.

item.title: 🌍 Economic strain mounts across emerging markets after two months of Iran war
item.description: Rising oil prices and supply disruptions are hitting developing economies hardest, with currency pressures and inflation concerns mounting across Asia, Africa, and Latin America.

item.title: 🛢️ IMO climate talks in London amid questions about Iran war's impact on energy transition
item.description: International Maritime Organization meeting convenes to discuss shipping emissions reductions, with delegates debating whether the conflict might accelerate or delay the shift away from fossil fuels.

item.title: 🎯 Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump at Washington press dinner
item.description: Prosecutors say the 31-year-old California man wanted to kill as many high-level officials as possible when he opened fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner attended by Trump.

item.title: 🌍 What to know about the US-Iran war after two months
item.description: A comprehensive overview of the conflict's key developments, from the initial strikes to the current standoff over the Strait of Hormuz and humanitarian toll across the region.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles arrives in US for delicate diplomatic mission
item.description: The British monarch began a four-day state visit to the United States amid tensions over the Iran war, with the king planning to address Congress and emphasize democratic values during his stay.

item.title: ⚔️ Oil gains 2% as Trump halts peace envoy and Tehran consults Moscow
item.description: Oil prices climbed after Trump ordered peace envoys home from Pakistan, ending attempts at negotiations, while Iran's foreign minister met Putin in Russia for consultations on the conflict.
